overview.headline: 3 high-risk changes need review
summaryBullets: 7 clause changes detected across 4 modified, 2 added, and 1 removed sections.
High-risk movement appears in Sharing Information and Dispute Resolution.
The biggest themes are Data sharing expansion plus Arbitration or class action.
Review the highlighted clauses before accepting the new terms, especially where rights or data use expanded.
whyMatters: Broader sharing language can materially change how personal data moves outside the service.
Dispute-resolution changes can limit how users or customers challenge the company.
Payment and renewal changes can create direct financial impact for the customer.
Retention changes alter how long sensitive information stays available.
The language changed, but it does not strongly match a high-signal legal or privacy risk pattern.
overview.disclaimer: Informational output only. TermsWatch surfaces change intelligence and risk signals, not legal advice.

changes.heading: Sharing Information
changes.beforeText: Sharing Information We do not sell personal information. We share data with service providers only to support operations on our behalf.
changes.afterText: Sharing Information We may share personal information with affiliates, analytics partners, and advertising partners to measure campaigns and improve recommendations. We do not sell personal information for money.
changes.whyItMatters: Broader sharing language can materially change how personal data moves outside the service.
changes.summary: The clause was broadened with additional language, suggesting expanded scope or new conditions.

changes.heading: Dispute Resolution
changes.afterText: Dispute Resolution Any dispute will be resolved through binding arbitration on an individual basis, and users waive participation in class actions.
changes.whyItMatters: Dispute-resolution changes can limit how users or customers challenge the company.
changes.summary: This language appears in the new version and creates a fresh obligation, permission, or disclosure.

changes.heading: Billing
changes.afterText: Billing Paid plans automatically renew unless canceled before the renewal date. Fees are non-refundable except where required by law.
changes.whyItMatters: Payment and renewal changes can create direct financial impact for the customer.
changes.summary: This language appears in the new version and creates a fresh obligation, permission, or disclosure.

changes.heading: Retention
changes.beforeText: Retention We keep account data for as long as the account remains active and remove deleted account data within 30 days.
changes.afterText: Retention We may retain account data for as long as needed for legal, security, analytics, and backup purposes, even after account closure.
changes.whyItMatters: Retention changes alter how long sensitive information stays available.
changes.summary: The clause was broadened with additional language, suggesting expanded scope or new conditions.

changes.heading: Data We Collect
changes.beforeText: Data We Collect We collect account details, basic usage information, and device identifiers needed to operate the service.
changes.afterText: Data We Collect We collect account details, device identifiers, approximate location, and product interaction data to operate and improve the service.
changes.whyItMatters: The language changed, but it does not strongly match a high-signal legal or privacy risk pattern.
changes.summary: The clause was broadened with additional language, suggesting expanded scope or new conditions.

changes.heading: How We Use Data
changes.beforeText: How We Use Data We use personal information to provide the service, secure accounts, and send service updates.
changes.afterText: How We Use Data We use personal information to provide the service, personalize the product experience, train internal models, and send service updates.
changes.whyItMatters: The language changed, but it does not strongly match a high-signal legal or privacy risk pattern.
changes.summary: The clause was broadened with additional language, suggesting expanded scope or new conditions.

changes.heading: Disputes
changes.beforeText: Disputes Disputes may be brought in local courts where required by law.
changes.whyItMatters: The language changed, but it does not strongly match a high-signal legal or privacy risk pattern.
changes.summary: This language existed before but no longer appears, which may reduce a user protection or a provider commitment.
